questions.question: Who is allowed to create a new form in Notion?

options.text: Only members on the Enterprise plan
options.explanation: Form creation is not limited to Enterprise; all members on all plans can create forms.

options.text: Only workspace owners
options.explanation: Workspace owners can create forms, but any member can as well.

options.text: All members on all Notion plans
options.explanation: The article states that all members on all plans can create and use forms.

options.text: Only users with a paid plan
options.explanation: Free plan users can also create forms; the ability isn’t restricted to paid plans.

questions.question: Which of the following actions can only be performed on Notion’s desktop or web apps, not on mobile?

options.text: Filling out a shared form
options.explanation: Forms can be filled out on mobile devices.

options.text: Viewing form responses
options.explanation: Viewing responses works on mobile as long as you have access.

options.text: Creating a new form
options.explanation: The article notes that creating a new form is only possible on desktop and web.

options.text: Copying the form link
options.explanation: Copying the link can be done from mobile as well.

questions.question: What property is automatically added to a database when you create a form from scratch?

options.text: Created by
options.explanation: Created by is optional; the automatic property is Respondent.

options.text: Respondent
options.explanation: A Respondent property is created automatically to capture respondents’ names.

options.text: Form owner
options.explanation: There is no Form owner property mentioned.

options.text: Submission date
options.explanation: Submission date is not automatically added; only Respondent is.

questions.question: If you want to add a form to an existing database, what level of access must you have to that database?

options.text: Can view
options.explanation: Can view lets you see the database but not add a form.

options.text: Can comment
options.explanation: Comment access does not permit creating views.

options.text: Full access
options.explanation: The article explicitly requires Full access to create a form from an existing database.

options.text: Can edit content
options.explanation: Can edit content is insufficient for adding a new view; Full access is required.

questions.question: Which plan(s) provide access to conditional logic in forms?

options.text: Free plan
options.explanation: Conditional logic is not available on the Free plan.

options.text: Personal plan
options.explanation: Personal plans do not include conditional logic.

options.text: Business and Enterprise plans
options.explanation: The article states conditional logic is only available on Business and Enterprise plans.

options.text: All paid plans
options.explanation: Only Business and Enterprise, not all paid plans, have this feature.

questions.question: When you change a question’s text in the form builder, what happens to the corresponding database property?

options.text: The property remains unchanged; you must rename it manually.
options.explanation: The property is automatically synced with the form question.

options.text: A new property is created, leaving the old one untouched.
options.explanation: Changing a question updates the existing property, not creates a new one.

options.text: The property is renamed to match the new question text.
options.explanation: The article explains that editing a question renames the associated property.

options.text: The property is deleted and a placeholder is added.
options.explanation: No deletion occurs; only renaming happens.

questions.question: What access level is required to change the sharing settings of a form?

options.text: Can comment access
options.explanation: Comment access does not allow changing sharing settings.

options.text: View access
options.explanation: View access only lets you see the form, not modify sharing.

options.text: Full access to the connected database
options.explanation: The article notes you need Full access to modify sharing settings.

options.text: Can edit content access
options.explanation: Can edit content is insufficient for sharing settings; Full access is required.

questions.question: If a form is shared with "Anyone on the web with link" and the workspace is on a paid plan, which option can the form owner toggle?

options.text: Allow respondents to edit other users’ submissions
options.explanation: This setting is unrelated to the paid‑plan toggle mentioned.

options.text: Enable or disable Notion branding on the form
options.explanation: The article says paid‑plan users can toggle Notion branding for web‑shared forms.

options.text: Require a Notion account to submit responses
options.explanation: Anyone on the web can submit without a Notion account.

options.text: Set a password for the form link
options.explanation: Password protection is not a feature described for Notion forms.

questions.question: Where are form responses stored by default, and how can they be analyzed?

options.text: In a dedicated Form view that can be exported as CSV
options.explanation: Form view cannot be exported; responses are stored in a Table view.

options.text: In a Table view named "Responses", which can be filtered, sorted, or visualized with chart views
options.explanation: The article states responses are stored in a Table view called Responses and can be analyzed via filters, sorts, and charts.

options.text: In a hidden page only accessible to the form creator
options.explanation: Responses are not hidden; they reside in a normal Table view.

options.text: In a separate Notion workspace linked to the form
options.explanation: Responses stay within the same database, not a separate workspace.

questions.question: What steps must a workspace owner on the Enterprise plan follow to prevent members from publishing forms to the web?

options.text: Go to Settings → Security and toggle on "Disable publishing sites, forms, and public links"
options.explanation: The article outlines this exact process for Enterprise owners.

options.text: Delete all existing public forms manually from each database
options.explanation: While deletion removes current forms, the setting prevents future publishing.

options.text: Change the workspace’s default sharing permissions to "Members only"
options.explanation: There is no global default sharing setting; the specific security toggle is required.

options.text: Upgrade every member to a Business plan to restrict web publishing
options.explanation: Plan upgrades do not control publishing; the security toggle does.
